title: Anticoagulation in AF: DOAC vs warfarin
key_recommendations: DOACs (apixaban, rivaroxaban, dabigatran, edoxaban) are preferred over warfarin in non-valvular AF except in mechanical valves, moderate-severe mitral stenosis, or eGFR <15.
Apixaban dose reduction (2.5 mg BID) if two of: age >=80, weight <=60 kg, creatinine >=1.5 mg/dL.
Rivaroxaban contraindicated if CrCl <15. Dose reduce to 15 mg daily if CrCl 15-50.
Switching from warfarin to DOAC: stop warfarin, start DOAC when INR < 2.0.
CHADS2-VASc score 2+ in men, 3+ in women, supports anticoagulation regardless of choice.
